standard: OWASP Top 10 Web Application Security Risks 2025

mapping.name: Broken Access Control
mapping.description: Restrictions on authenticated users are not properly enforced. Flaws allow access to unauthorized functions or data.

mapping.name: Cryptographic Failures
mapping.description: Failures related to cryptography, often leading to sensitive data exposure or system compromise.

mapping.name: Injection
mapping.description: User-supplied data is not validated, filtered, or sanitized. Hostile data used in interpreter calls.

mapping.name: Insecure Design
mapping.description: Risks related to design and architectural flaws. Missing or ineffective security controls.

mapping.name: Security Misconfiguration
mapping.description: Missing hardening, improperly configured permissions, unnecessary features enabled, default accounts.

mapping.name: Vulnerable and Outdated Components
mapping.description: Using components with known vulnerabilities, outdated or unsupported software.

mapping.name: Identification and Authentication Failures
mapping.description: Confirmation of user identity, authentication, and session management vulnerabilities.

mapping.name: Software and Data Integrity Failures
mapping.description: Code and infrastructure that does not protect against integrity violations.

mapping.name: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
mapping.description: Insufficient logging, detection, monitoring, and active response.

mapping.name: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
mapping.description: Web application fetches a remote resource without validating the user-supplied URL.
